nodes.context: Yakuza organization headquartered in Kurume, Fukuoka, Japan; involved in drug trafficking.
Yakuza group based in Tagawa, known as Japan's Methlord in the Showa era; crushed by Taishu-kai.
Yakuza syndicate based in northern Kyushu; crushed Sadaoka-gumi.
Yakuza group based in Okawa; disbanded.
Yakuza group based in Kumamoto; disbanded.
Largest yakuza syndicate in Japan; rival of Dojin-kai.
Kanto-based large yakuza syndicate; had conflict with Dojin-kai in 1983.
Northern Kyushu-based yakuza syndicate; member of Yonsha-kai.
Northern Kyushu-based yakuza syndicate; member of Yonsha-kai.
Anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation of northern Kyushu yakuza syndicates.
Splinter group of Dojin-kai; aligned with Yamaguchi-gumi.
Mexican drug cartel; ally of Dojin-kai per infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai is noted for having been isolated in the yakuza world...
edges.context: Dojin-kai fought the Yamaguchi-gumi in the Yama-Michi War (1986-1987) and considers them rivals.

edges.evidence_quote: Another notable one is a brief conflict that occurred in 1983, with the Kanto-based large yakuza syndicate Sumiyoshi Union
edges.context: Dojin-kai had a brief conflict with Sumiyoshi Union in 1983.

edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai again started a blatantly vicious conflict with its splinter group, Kyushu Seido-kai, in 2006.
edges.context: Dojin-kai has been in a violent conflict with its splinter group Kyushu Seido-kai since 2006.

edges.evidence_quote: They allegedly aligned themselves with the Yamaguchi-gumi, the Dojin-kai's rival and the largest yakuza syndicate in Japan.
edges.context: Kyushu Seido-kai aligned with Yamaguchi-gumi during the conflict with Dojin-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: The Dojin-kai is a member of an anti-Yamaguchi fraternal federation, the Yonsha-kai, formed with three other northern-Kyushu based independent yakuza syndicates, the Kudo-kai, Taishu-kai and Kumamoto-kai.
edges.context: Dojin-kai is a member of the Yonsha-kai federation.
